meta.ground_truth_source: Ochoa Chaves et al. (2023). IDB Technical Note. doi:10.18235/0005019
secondary_sources: PAHO Costa Rica interoperability gap documentation
World Bank PforR 2024 (Costa Rica CCSS EDUS)
Estrategia Nacional de Salud Digital Costa Rica 2023-2030

expected_block1.quality_coverage_gap_note: Only 4 of 94 health areas achieve >80% quality prenatal care (NDHS 2023-2030)

expected_block2.hmis_platform: SIVEI (under Ministry of Health, separate from CCSS)
expected_block2.poc_hmis_integration_note: EDUS (CCSS) and SIVEI (Ministry of Health) not automatically connected. Documented in PAHO Tier 1 source.
expected_block2.system_in_transition_detail: SNOMED CT integration active; IAFA expansion Phase 2 Sept 2025

key_insights: EDUS not in any WHO registry — found via IDB case study
EDUS-SIVEI gap documented in PAHO Tier 1, not in CCSS website
Quality gap (4/94 areas) redefines DAK use case from coverage to quality standardization
DAK ANC in Costa Rica = quality decision-support, not basic data capture
expected_moh_questions_themes: EDUS IT team capacity for DAK integration in 2026-2027 roadmap
EDUS-SIVEI data flow existence for ANC indicators
Impact of active SNOMED CT integration on ANC data elements
Whether CCSS has reviewed WHO ANC DAK content standards
How the 90 underperforming health areas are being supported
